---
title: "Measure-valued processes"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: cosmo
    css: style.css
---

\newcommand{\C}{\mathbb{C}}
\newcommand{\R}{\mathbb{R}}
\newcommand{\Z}{\mathbb{Z}}
\newcommand{\N}{\mathbb{N}}
\newcommand{\<}{\left<}
\newcommand{\>}{\right>}

## Hochberg

  - $S$, a locally compact complete separable (Polish) space
  
  - $M(S)$, space of all bounded Radon measures on $S$
  
    - includes a metric that also makes it a Polish space with a weak topology (weak conv of msrs)
  
  - $E\subset_{cl}M(S)$, $B(E)=\sigma$-algebra of Borel sets

  - $C_b(E)$, space of cont-bounded fcts on $E$ (fcts of msrs)

  - $L_\infty(E)$, space of bounded-mbl fcts on $E$

  - $\Omega_C^E=C(\R_+,E)$, continuous $E$-valued fcts

  - $\Omega_D^E=D(\R_+,E)$, RCLL $E$-valued fcts 

$$X:[0,\infty]\times\Omega^E\rightarrow E, \ \ \ X(t,\omega)\equiv\omega(t), \ \ \omega(t)\in\Omega^E \ \forall t\geq0$$

  - The law of $X$ is given by a mbl mapping $\mu\rightarrow P_\mu$ taking $E$ into the space of probability msrs on $\Omega^E$, $P(\Omega^E)$.

  - $P(\Omega^E)$ also has the weak topology
